name: Linux Infrastructure Monitoring
author: OpsDB Platform
description: Monitor Linux servers via SSH including CPU usage, memory utilisation, disk I/O, network throughput, and filesystem capacity.

linux.cpu_memory.description: CPU usage percent and memory utilisation from /proc/stat and /proc/meminfo.
linux.cpu_memory.command: python3 -c "import json;s=open('/proc/stat').readline().split();idle,total=int(s[4]),sum(int(x) for x in s[1:]);m={l.split(':')[0]:int(l.split()[1])*1024 for l in open('/proc/meminfo') if ':' in l};avail=m.get('MemAvailable',m.get('MemFree',0));print(json.dumps({'cpu_usage_percent':round((1-idle/total)*100,1),'memory_used_percent':round((m['MemTotal']-avail)/m['MemTotal']*100,1),'memory_total_bytes':m['MemTotal'],'memory_available_bytes':avail}))"

linux.disk_io.description: Maximum disk usage percent across all mounted filesystems.
linux.disk_io.command: python3 -c "import json,subprocess;lines=subprocess.check_output(['df','-B1','--output=source,size,used,avail,pcent,target']).decode().strip().split('\n')[1:];pcts=[int(p[4].rstrip('%')) for p in [l.split() for l in lines] if len(p)==6 and p[5].startswith('/') and not p[5].startswith('/proc') and not p[5].startswith('/sys')];print(json.dumps({'disk_used_percent':max(pcts) if pcts else 0}))"

linux.network.description: Total rx/tx bytes across non-loopback interfaces from /proc/net/dev.
linux.network.command: python3 -c "import json;lines=open('/proc/net/dev').read().strip().split('\n')[2:];rx=tx=0;[None for l in lines if ':' in l and l.split(':')[0].strip()!='lo' and (globals().update({'rx':rx+int(l.split(':')[1].split()[0]),'tx':tx+int(l.split(':')[1].split()[8])}) or True)];print(json.dumps({'network_rx_bytes':rx,'network_tx_bytes':tx}))"

linux_cpu_usage_high.title: CPU Usage High
linux_cpu_usage_high.recommendation: Identify high-CPU processes, check for runaway cron jobs, and review scheduling.

linux_disk_usage_high.title: Disk Usage High
linux_disk_usage_high.recommendation: Free disk space, archive or delete old logs, and review retention policies.

linux_memory_pressure_high.title: Memory Pressure High
linux_memory_pressure_high.recommendation: Review memory-heavy processes, check for memory leaks, and consider adding swap or RAM.
